description:
Audit existing Diátaxis documentation for drift and apply fixes, one item per 
loop/judge cycle, scored by IMPACT PER DIÁTAXIS TYPE. Spawns: root epic -> a 
single audit spawner (detect drift across tutorials / how-to / reference / 
explanation, score by impact, write _UpdateQueue.md) -> one bead per queue item 
created at runtime (UPDATE / NEW / REMOVE / VERIFY / LEAKAGE) -> a finalize bead 
gated on all of them that archives the queue into _AuditLog.md. The Diátaxis 
sibling of flowdoc-maintain (ADR flowdoc-yuw / decisions/0001): it audits the 
Diátaxis tree produced by diataxis-generate, not the legacy features-not-files 
tree. Requires the matching DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md already exists (run 
diataxis-generate first). Run via 
'bd mol pour diataxis-maintain --var audience=maintainer|user'.

audience.description: maintainer = audit diataxis/maintainer/ (developer/contributor impact scoring); user = audit diataxis/user/ (end-user impact scoring + developer-leakage scan). The audience overlay constrains the SURFACE DETAIL a doc may contain; it never overrides a Diátaxis type's orientation when scoring or fixing.

steps.title: Diátaxis: maintain {{audience}} documentation
steps.description:
Umbrella for a Diátaxis maintenance pass ({{audience}} edition).

Diátaxis organises docs around FOUR USER NEEDS on a 2×2 compass
(research/tech/diataxis-documentation-framework-research.md; ADR flowdoc-yuw):
    - action  + acquisition  -> tutorial      (learning-oriented)
    - action  + application  -> how-to guide   (goal/task-oriented)
    - cognition + application -> reference      (information-oriented)
    - cognition + acquisition -> explanation    (understanding-oriented)
Drift and the fixes for it are scored BY TYPE, because each type carries a
different reliability contract (e.g. a broken tutorial step is critical; a
reference that no longer matches the product is critical; a missing explanation
is usually medium). This is the maintenance counterpart of diataxis-generate.

AUDIENCE MAP (read this first, every child obeys it):
  - maintainer -> audit diataxis/maintainer/ ; score by developer/contributor
    impact; implementation detail / source paths / code are allowed in docs.
  - user       -> audit diataxis/user/       ; score by end-user impact; also
    strip any developer content (source paths, code, internal/localhost/staging
    URLs, build/test/dev-setup) that leaked into user docs.

Two authored children: 'audit' (detect drift + spawn one fix bead per finding)
and 'finalize' (archive the queue once every fix is closed). Work the leaves,
not the epic. Precondition: DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md must already exist (i.e.
diataxis-generate was poured for this audience first).

steps.title: Diátaxis {{audience}}: audit for drift by type, then spawn one fix bead per finding
steps.description:
AUDIT + FAN-OUT (one bead). Your bead id is shown as "Complete beads issue
<id>" at the top of this goal — call it $SELF.

AUDIENCE = {{audience}}.  DOCS_DIR = diataxis/maintainer/ if maintainer,
diataxis/user/ if user.
PRECONDITION: if DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md does not exist, stop and report that
diataxis-generate must be poured for this audience first — do NOT spawn
anything. DOCS_DIR/_DiataxisGuide.md (the authoring contract written by
diataxis-generate) is the source of truth for the compass + the four per-type
templates; NEW/UPDATE fixes must follow it.

== Part A: detect drift (one pass), TYPE-AWARE ==
1. Survey current code: list_files(".", recursive=True); read dependency
   manifests for version changes; read configs and entry points; note new /
   renamed / deleted files, routes, commands, exports, public APIs. You are
   re-checking USER NEEDS against reality, not doing a file inventory.
2. Load doc state: read DOCS_DIR/_Manifest.md (items grouped by type) and each
   doc under tutorials/ how-to/ reference/ explanation/.
3. Run detection strategies, framed per Diátaxis type:
   - TUTORIAL reliability check (the strictest contract): re-trace every
     documented step against current entry points / commands / config /
     prerequisites. Tutorials "aspire to perfect reliability", so ANY step
     that no longer works exactly as written, or a prerequisite that changed,
     is drift of the highest severity.
   - HOW-TO task check: does each documented recipe still accomplish its real
     task? Do the commands / flags / options it uses still exist and behave as
     described? Are there new REQUIRED steps? Is a 'fix X' guide stale?
   - REFERENCE accuracy check: compare every described command / variable /
     parameter / field / signature / return value against the actual product
     surface. Reference must "describe and only describe" the machinery as it
     IS — any mismatch, removal, or signature change is high severity; an
     undocumented new item is a coverage gap (-> NEW reference).
   - EXPLANATION freshness check: is there a significant new concept, design
     decision, or architecture change with no explanation? Has an existing
     'why' / tradeoff / alternative gone stale or contradictory? (Explanation
     is understanding-oriented and not tied to one task, so it drifts slower
     and usually scores medium.)
   - COVERAGE / compass check: a genuinely new user need with no doc in ANY
     type -> NEW (run the compass to classify which type it belongs in). A
     documented capability that was removed from the product -> REMOVE.
   - CROSS-CUTTING: dependency/tech-stack drift vs any reference/explanation
     that states the stack; config-key drift vs config/.env; broken intra-doc
     links; empty type folders that should not exist.
   - (user edition ONLY) Developer-leakage scan of existing user docs: source
     paths (src/, *.ts, *.py), shell/install commands (npm/git/make/uv),
     internal URLs (localhost/127.0.0.1/staging/.dev), class/function names.
4. Score each finding by impact, WEIGHTED BY TYPE, and bucket it:
   - tutorial: broken/incorrect step +10, changed prerequisite +8, wrong
     expected-result text +5, 'where next' link rot +2.
   - how-to: documented task step broken +8, command/flag moved or renamed +8,
     new required step +7, option/behaviour changed +5, stale variation +3.
   - reference: described item no longer matches product (signature/param/
     field/return/command changed or removed) +10, undocumented new public
     item +5, ordering no longer mirrors product +2.
   - explanation: significant new concept/design with no explanation +5,
     outdated rationale or tradeoff +4, minor stale context +2.
   - cross-cutting: feature removed-but-documented +7, dependency/tech-stack
     drift +5, config drift +4, broken intra-doc link +4, (user) leakage +4,
     wording/typo +1.
   Tiers: >=8 critical, 4-7 medium, 1-3 low, 0 verify-only.
5. Write DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md: audit summary, a findings-by-type table
   (Tutorial / How-to / Reference / Explanation / Cross-cutting), counters,
   and the queue ordered by score, each line tagged with an action AND its
   type:
     "- [ ] U001 | UPDATE | reference | [Name](reference/name.md) | <what drifted>".
   Append a dated entry to DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md.

== Part B: fan out — ONE bead per queue item (deadlock-safe contract) ==
For EACH queue item create exactly one child bead:
  bd create --type=task --priority=<1 if critical, 2 if medium, 3 if low> --parent=$SELF --title='Diátaxis {{audience}} <ACTION> (<type>): <Name>' --description='<ACTION> the <type> doc for audience={{audience}}, staying within that
       Diátaxis type''s orientation (tutorials minimise explanation; how-to
       stays task-focused; reference describes only; explanation may give the
       why/tradeoffs). Follow DOCS_DIR/_DiataxisGuide.md.
       UPDATE = re-read the source, fix the drifted sections in the doc''s
         single type orientation, add a > [!NOTE] Last Updated stamp.
       NEW = first RUN THE COMPASS (action/cognition? acquisition/application?)
         to confirm the type, create the matching <type>/ folder if it does not
         yet exist (lazy — no empty buckets), write the doc from that type''s
         template in DOCS_DIR/_DiataxisGuide.md, and add it to _Manifest.md.
       REMOVE = delete the doc, drop it from _Manifest.md and index.md, fix
         inbound links, and delete the type folder if it is now empty.
       VERIFY = re-read source, confirm the doc is accurate, mark it verified
         (no rewrite).
       LEAKAGE = replace developer content with user-facing equivalents.
       Then tick this item -> [x] in _UpdateQueue.md and bump the counters.
       Acceptance: action applied, doc stays within its single Diátaxis type,
       _UpdateQueue.md + _Manifest.md updated, links valid, ({{audience}}=user)
       no developer leakage introduced.'
WIRING RULES — obey exactly to stay deadlock-free:
  - Parent EVERY spawned bead to $SELF (so the finalize fan-out gate counts it).
  - No dependency from any fix bead to the finalize bead.
  - No blocks edges between fix beads — they are independent/parallel.
  - Do NOT create your own finalize/rollup bead; one already exists and waits.
  - Spawn AT LEAST ONE child. If there is genuinely no drift, spawn a single
    VERIFY bead ("confirm docs current, no drift found") so the gate resolves.
  - Do NOT close any bead — the LLM judges close. (bead-chain blocks bd close.)

Stop after writing the queue + spawning. Summarize findings by type and how
many fix beads you spawned.

steps.title: Diátaxis {{audience}}: finalize maintenance (archive queue, compass review)
steps.description:
FINALIZE the {{audience}} maintenance pass. You become ready only once 'audit'
and EVERY fix bead it spawned are closed (fan-out gate).

DOCS_DIR = diataxis/maintainer/ if maintainer, diataxis/user/ if user.
1. Confirm every item in DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md is checked [x]; fix counters.
2. Append a completion summary (updates / new / removed / verified, broken out
   by Diátaxis type) to DOCS_DIR/_AuditLog.md, then delete
   DOCS_DIR/_UpdateQueue.md.
3. Verify NO empty type folder exists: every one of tutorials/ how-to/
   reference/ explanation/ that is present holds >=1 real doc; delete any that
   ended up empty after REMOVEs (the Diátaxis anti-pattern guard).
4. Compass review: skim each touched doc and confirm it stays within its single
   Diátaxis orientation — tutorials didn't bloat into explanation, reference
   stayed describe-only, how-to stayed task-focused, explanation didn't become
   a walkthrough. Note (do NOT silently rewrite) any clear violation so the
   judges can see it; the compass is a SHOULD.
5. Verify _Manifest.md, index.md, every present section listing, and all
   intra-doc links are consistent after the additions/removals.
6. (user edition only) Grep the docs for developer leakage — source paths,
   shell/install commands (src/ | npm  | localhost), staging URLs, class/
   function names — and replace with user-facing equivalents.
READ-ONLY source. Output only under DOCS_DIR. Do NOT close any bead yourself.
